pages.full_text:
reached our room at 3am. In the [?a+d] ambulance John had a pill up his bum + oxygen in his nose + stopped feeling sick + was fine. We were finally let out at 1.0 pm.
__Monday 3rd August__
	Simon + the boys slept on the carpark + shopped at a supermarket.
	We caught the 4.0 ferry to __Ornes__ + reached a marvellous stave church
[Photo caption: A model of two Edwardians on their Harley Davigon at the Solomon museum]
We could climb up into the gallery + see beautifully carved capitals. Three families had private box pews, one of them still lives in the village - Bugge. Stave churches are built with very strong vertical pillars
photo_captions: A model of two Edwardians on their Harley Davigon at the Solomon museum
pages.notes: Page is a continuation — text begins mid-sentence ('reached our room at 3am'). No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The word after 'the' before 'ambulance' is unclear — possibly an abbreviation or word like 'a+d' or 'A&E'; written as '[?a+d]'. 'Harley Davigon' appears to be the diarist's spelling of 'Harley Davidson'. 'Solomon museum' may refer to a local museum; could alternatively be read as 'Solvorn museum' or similar Norwegian place — noted as uncertainty. 'Ornes' is the ferry destination; this is a place on the Sognefjord / Lustrafjord in Norway, consistent with Urnes Stave Church nearby (spelled 'Urnes' on the ticket on the right page). Year cannot be confirmed from this page; Norway trip within 1989–2003 range. Text continues at right edge of page (partially visible: 'in', 'vil[lage]', 'Th[e]', 'th[e]', 'ha[d]', 'the') — this is the gutter overlap and belongs to the right page.

pages.full_text:
[Glued-in ticket: URNES STAVKYRKJE / FORENINGEN TIL NORSKE FORTIDSMINNES- MERKERS BEVARING / Kr. 80,- / 027]

We found a beautiful valley to camp in, with a lake (drinking-water for the village, so no swimming) views of waterfalls. There was alot of rain in the night + the waterfalls increased in size; they haven't formed clefts in the hillside, they just pour out over the rocks.
__Skjolden__,** [?too] above the head of [?Luster] fjord

__Tues 4th [?August]__		__Borgsdalen__
	We left in a drizzle + went up a steep road - 8% for 10 km. It went on + on to a high area, Very barren + misty + mysterious; a crew of a glacier. Stupid
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 67 visible at top-right corner. A glued-in admission ticket for Urnes Stavkyrkje (Urnes Stave Church) is at the top of the page, printed text reads 'URNES STAVKYRKJE / FORENINGEN TIL NORSKE FORTIDSMINNES- MERKERS BEVARING / Kr. 80,- / 027'. 'Skjolden' is underlined and followed by '**' (two asterisks, possibly a rating) and a partially legible word that may be 'too' or 'just'; then 'above the head of [?Luster] fjord' — Luster/Lustrafjord is the correct geographic name for this area. 'Borgsdalen' is underlined and appears as a heading on the same line as the date. The month in 'Tues 4th [?August]' is partially unclear but consistent with 'Monday 3rd August' on the left page. 'a crew of a glacier' likely means 'a view of a glacier'. Text ends mid-sentence — continues on next spread. Year cannot be confirmed from page content; Norway camping trip within 1989–2003.
